body:
Hi Joan, quick nudge — we still need your evidence summary for the City Hall consultation pack before I send it to the printers. The 10 October session is creeping up and I want the boards proofed in good time.

No need to polish it, even a rough version of the feasibility figures works and I'll format it. Could you get it to me by Friday?

Tomás
snippet: Hi Joan, quick nudge — we still need your evidence summary for the City Hall con...
subject: Consultation pack — evidence summary

body:
Hey you! Are we still on for Saturday coffee at Kai? Feels like ages since we actually sat down — I've wedding stuff to offload on you and I need a break from Darragh's seating-plan spreadsheet.

10:30 as usual? I'll grab the window table if I'm in first.

Nina x

body:
Yes please, I need it. I'm off the rota this weekend so no 3am dashes for once.

10:30 works — get the window if you beat me there. Bring the seating plan, I live for that kind of chaos when it's not mine. I might be five minutes late, I want to swing by the market for Mam's birthday card first.

Joan
snippet: Yes please, I need it. I'm off the rota this weekend so no 3am dashes for once....

Perfect. Window table is mine then. And yes, bring opinions on table 6 — it's a war zone.

Can't wait. Mind yourself til then x

body:
Hi Joan,

I've read through your literature review draft — a really strong start, especially the framing of continuity-of-carer outcomes. Before module 3 in November I'd like to tighten two things: the inclusion criteria for the US comparison strand, and how you're handling the Boston exchange data alongside the Irish figures.

Could we find 30 minutes this week or early next? Happy to do it over Zoom around your clinic days.

Niamh
snippet:
Hi Joan,

I've read through your literature review draft — a really strong star...
subject: Lit review draft — feedback

body:
Hi Niamh,

Thank you — that's reassuring, the comparison strand is exactly where I've felt least sure. I take your point on the inclusion criteria; I think I've been too broad.

I'm in clinic Tuesday and Thursday but free Friday afternoon during my study block. Would 2pm Friday on Zoom suit? I'll send the revised criteria ahead so we can use the time on the substance.

Joan
snippet:
Hi Niamh,

Thank you — that's reassuring, the comparison strand is exactly wher...
subject: Re: Lit review draft — feedback

body:
Friday at 2 is grand. Send the revised criteria by Thursday evening and I'll come with comments.

One thing to mull beforehand: be ready to justify why the US mid-sized-city centres are a fair comparator for a Galway catchment. That's the question the examiners will go for.

Niamh
snippet: Friday at 2 is grand. Send the revised criteria by Thursday evening and I'll co...
subject: Re: Lit review draft — feedback

body:
That's the exact question Roisin put to me last month, so it's clearly the soft spot. I'll build the justification into the methods section rather than leaving it to defend live.

Revised criteria with you by Thursday. Thanks Niamh, this helps — see you Friday.

Joan
snippet: That's the exact question Roisin put to me last month, so it's clearly the soft...
subject: Re: Lit review draft — feedback

body:
Hey, are we still heading to Clifden Saturday to see your folks? If we leave around 9 we'll miss the worst of the N59. I can drive.

Your mam texted me (of course) asking if I'll bring sourdough — I'll do a gluten-free loaf Friday night so I'm not raiding her kitchen. Anything we need to pick up for your dad?

C
snippet: Hey, are we still heading to Clifden Saturday to see your folks? If we leave aro...
subject: Clifden Saturday

body:
Yes, 9 is good — I'm not on call so for once we're not at the mercy of the phone.

Bring the loaf, she'll be made up. Dad's knee has been stiff in the damp so don't let him carry anything; I'll have a quiet word. Can you grab his prescription from the chemist on the way out of town? I'll text you the name.

Joan
snippet: Yes, 9 is good — I'm not on call so for once we're not at the mercy of the phon...

body:
Done, I'll collect it before we go. I'll keep an eye on your dad and the heavy lifting — you know what he's like.

Should we stay the Sunday or head back Saturday evening? Forecast looks soft Sunday, could be a nice Western Way stretch if we stay over.
snippet: Done, I'll collect it before we go. I'll keep an eye on your dad and the heavy l...

body:
Let's stay over — I'd love a walk if the weather holds, and Mam will be thrilled to have us for the Sunday lie-in and a fry (gluten-free for you, she's got the hang of it now).

I'll square it with her on the call tonight. Pack layers and the rain gear — it's Connemara, it'll do all four seasons before lunch.

J
snippet: Let's stay over — I'd love a walk if the weather holds, and Mam will be thrille...

Perfect. I'll pack for monsoon and sun both. Loaf's proving already.

See you after work — I'll be the one with the bread and an unreasonable amount of optimism about the forecast. x

Perfect. I'll pack for monsoon and sun both. Loaf's proving already.

body:
Joan,

Two things before the week runs away. First — your advanced NRP recert practical is booked for 14 January in the sim suite; I've put you on the prep list. Do the scenario refreshers over the autumn, don't leave it to Christmas.

Second, the birth centre proposal: I think you should get ahead of Roisin's intervention-threshold concerns rather than wait for them at the consultation. Want to sit down before then and war-game the hard questions?

Fionnuala
snippet:
Joan,

Two things before the week runs away. First — your advanced NRP recert p...
subject: NRP recert + proposal prep

body:
Thank you Fionnuala — yes to both. I'll start the scenario refreshers in October once the consultation's behind me, and I'll log them as I go for the portfolio.

And you're right about Roisin. I'd rather have the answers ready than be caught flat at City Hall. Could we grab an hour the week before the 10th? I'll bring the evidence map and the bits I'm least sure of.

Joan
snippet: Thank you Fionnuala — yes to both. I'll start the scenario refreshers in Octobe...
subject: Re: NRP recert + proposal prep

body:
Hi Joan,

Where we've landed on the postnatal home-visit pathway: I've pulled the visit-timing data for the last two quarters and there's a clear gap around day 5 for the rural cases out past Oughterard. I think that's our strongest argument for the rescheduling.

The Director wants the findings in December, so could you review the draft schedule I shared and flag anything that won't work operationally with the community rota?

Fionnuala
snippet:
Hi Joan,

Where we've landed on the postnatal home-visit pathway: I've pulled t...
subject: Postnatal pathway — draft schedule

body:
Hi Fionnuala,

That day-5 rural gap matches exactly what I see on the ground — the distances out west make the current timing unrealistic on an on-call week. Good to have the data behind it now.

One operational flag: any rescheduling has to survive a midwife being pulled to a birth mid-round, so let's build slack into the day rather than a tight sequence. I'll mark up the draft and send it back Thursday. Shall we sync before the December presentation?
snippet:
Hi Fionnuala,

That day-5 rural gap matches exactly what I see on the ground — the d...
subject: Re: Postnatal pathway — draft schedule

body:
That makes sense — I'll add buffer slots to each round so a birth doesn't collapse the whole day.

Yes, let's meet in late November to rehearse the presentation for the Director. I'll find a slot that dodges your on-call week. Thanks Joan, this is coming together.
snippet: That makes sense — I'll add buffer slots to each round so a birth doesn't colla...
subject: Re: Postnatal pathway — draft schedule
